title: Mastering Minecraft Events
description: A complete, deeply explained guide to events including setup, listeners, priorities, async handling, and real debugging workflow.

lessons.title: Introduction to Events (FULL GUIDE)
lessons.content:
# Introduction to Events (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: WHERE THIS CODE GOES

Create a new class:

JoinListener.java

Inside your plugin package.

---

## Step 2: REQUIRED STRUCTURE

```java
public class JoinListener implements Listener {

    @EventHandler
    public void onJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
        event.getPlayer().sendMessage("Welcome to the server!");
    }

}
```

---

## Step 3: FULL BREAKDOWN (LINE BY LINE)

implements Listener
→ tells Spigot this class listens to events

@EventHandler
→ REQUIRED annotation or event will NOT run

PlayerJoinEvent
→ specific event type

onJoin(...)
→ method name can be anything

event.getPlayer()
→ gets player who triggered event

---

## Step 4: WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS (IMPORTANT)

1. Player joins server
2. Server fires PlayerJoinEvent
3. Your listener catches it
4. Your code runs

---

## Step 5: COMMON BEGINNER MISTAKES

❌ Forgetting @EventHandler → nothing runs
❌ Wrong event import → code "works" but never fires
❌ Not registering listener (VERY COMMON)

---

## Mini Challenge
Change message to show player name

## Recap
Event = trigger → listener → code executes

lessons.title: Registering Event Listeners (CRITICAL)
lessons.content:
# Registering Events (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: WHY THIS MATTERS

If you DON'T register your listener...

➡ NOTHING WILL WORK

---

## Step 2: WHERE TO PUT THIS

Main class (your plugin class)

Example:

Main.java

---

## Step 3: CODE

```java
@Override
public void onEnable() {
    getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new JoinListener(), this);
}
```

---

## Step 4: FULL BREAKDOWN

onEnable()
→ runs when plugin starts

getServer().getPluginManager()
→ gets system controlling events

registerEvents(listener, plugin)
→ connects your listener to server

new JoinListener()
→ creates instance of your class

this
→ your plugin instance

---

## Step 5: HOW TO TEST

Add this:

```java
getLogger().info("Events registered");
```

Check console on startup

---

## Step 6: COMMON MISTAKE

❌ Putting registerEvents in wrong class
❌ Forgetting to restart server

---

## Mini Challenge
Register a second listener

lessons.title: Player Events (DEEP EXPLANATION)

quiz.question: Which event runs when player joins?

lessons.title: Block Events (CONTROL GAMEPLAY)
lessons.content:
# Block Events (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: CODE

```java
@EventHandler
public void onBreak(BlockBreakEvent event) {
    if (event.getBlock().getType().toString().contains("DIAMOND")) {
        event.setCancelled(true);
    }
}
```

---

## Step 2: BREAKDOWN

getBlock()
→ gets block broken

getType()
→ gets material

setCancelled(true)
→ STOPS action

---

## Step 3: WHAT HAPPENS

Player tries to break diamond block
→ event cancelled
→ block stays

---

## Step 4: IMPORTANT CONCEPT

Events can be:

✔ Modified
✔ Cancelled
✔ Observed

---

## Step 5: COMMON MISTAKE

❌ Forgetting setCancelled
→ logic runs but does nothing

---

## Mini Challenge
Only allow breaking stone

lessons.title: Chat Events (ASYNC WARNING)
lessons.content:
# Chat Events (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: CODE

```java
@EventHandler
public void onChat(AsyncPlayerChatEvent event) {
    event.setMessage("[Modified] " + event.getMessage());
}
```

---

## Step 2: IMPORTANT CONCEPT

Async = NOT main server thread

---

## Step 3: WHAT THIS MEANS

❌ DO NOT:
- Access world
- Teleport players

✔ SAFE:
- Modify message

---

## Step 4: WHAT HAPPENS

Player sends message
→ plugin edits it

---

## Step 5: COMMON MISTAKE

❌ Doing heavy logic → lag or crashes

---

## Mini Challenge
Add player prefix

lessons.title: Event Priority (ADVANCED CONTROL)
lessons.content:
# Event Priority (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: CODE

```java
@EventHandler(priority = EventPriority.HIGH, ignoreCancelled = true)
public void onBreak(BlockBreakEvent event) {}
```

---

## Step 2: PRIORITIES ORDER

LOW → NORMAL → HIGH → HIGHEST → MONITOR

---

## Step 3: WHAT THIS MEANS

LOW = runs first
MONITOR = runs last (do NOT modify)

---

## Step 4: ignoreCancelled

Skips events already cancelled

---

## Step 5: REAL USE CASE

- LOW: modify behaviour early
- HIGH: override others
- MONITOR: logging only

---

## Mini Challenge
Log only cancelled events

lessons.title: Testing & Debugging (REAL DEV WORKFLOW)
lessons.content:
# Debugging Events (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: ALWAYS USE LOGS

```java
getLogger().info("Event fired");
```

---

## Step 2: DEBUG FLOW

1. Trigger event
2. Check console
3. Add logs
4. Narrow problem

---

## Step 3: COMMON ISSUES

❌ Not registered
❌ Wrong import
❌ Plugin not reloaded
❌ Event cancelled elsewhere

---

## Step 4: PRO TIP

Check if cancelled:

```java
if (event.isCancelled()) {
    getLogger().info("Cancelled by another plugin");
}
```

---

## Step 5: WHAT PROS DO

- Test ONE thing at a time
- Log EVERYTHING
- Never guess

---

## Mini Challenge
Log player name on every event
